intro: Porcelia triloba, also known as "Tucuma", is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Porcelia triloba include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Porcelia triloba include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
